Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne on CentOS 8.3/x86_64 uploaded by Trevis Autotest from trevis-107vm1: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/ba33f262-4509-4ba2-9783-95c7ede73bb8 ran 5 tests.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

looks OK. what was happening exactly? LOV_ALL_STRIPES is 0xffff, so the loop would stop?

Patch Set 1:

I don't know exactly the mechanism, but I just saw this in EX-3205 and I recall seeing a similar case where there are timeouts or some other error being returned (-EINVAL or whatever) and the code just keeps looping forever (not even 2^32 limit).  The default for "lfs df" was changed to not print errors because sites configure with gaps in the OST index, so it is too noisy. 

So this isn't fixing the root cause, but at least it eventually unblocks and returns an error code to the caller.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

* Code-Review-1 by WC Checkpatch (377)
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 41703e9b5fe7061b313805384b08372d4fe82cec by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-15484 tests: Increase timeout to wait for child

commit.subject: LU-15647 utils: 'lfs df' shouldn't loop forever
commit.message:
LU-15647 utils: 'lfs df' shouldn't loop forever

Don't loop forever in "lfs df" if there is a problem fetching
the target statfs information from the kernel and -ENODEV is
not returned for whatever reason.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I5110c7246cf53eb5c4d59424a44b73b9d23ebbe5
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/46813
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
